reviewed_sources.name: Distribution manifest policy surface
reviewed_sources.notes: The policy, checker, documentation, and shared library must remain declared in source, target, readiness, script, and copy-rule projections.
reviewed_sources.lifecycle_reason: Generation is the propagation boundary where repository-owned governance becomes available in initialized projects.
reviewed_sources.expected_behavior: Every newly generated project receives the retention policy, checker, documentation, and shared retention library through declared manifest projections.
reviewed_sources.negative_or_reverse_behavior: A missing or stale declaration must fail manifest or generated-project verification instead of silently omitting governance files.

reviewed_sources.name: Verification command integration
reviewed_sources.notes: The retention checker is a named command and a mandatory pre-runtime verification step.
reviewed_sources.lifecycle_reason: Pre-runtime validation is the earliest bounded point that can reject duplicate or oversized future evidence before closure.
reviewed_sources.expected_behavior: The standard pre-runtime command invokes strict evidence-retention validation and propagates its non-zero exit status.
reviewed_sources.negative_or_reverse_behavior: An invalid retention state must stop pre-runtime verification; no alternate standard command may bypass the gate.

reviewed_sources.name: Retention policy evaluation
reviewed_sources.processing_path_name: Forward-only retention classification and budget evaluation
reviewed_sources.notes: The library classifies current-task raw evidence and runtime archives, detects duplicates, and enforces configured budgets without mutating files.
reviewed_sources.lifecycle_reason: Classification and evaluation implement the central governance transition from observed files to pass-or-fail findings.
reviewed_sources.expected_behavior: Evidence for tasks at or after 1.118 is checked for one final durable runtime, forbidden aggregate logs, exact raw duplicates, per-file limits, and task-total limits.
reviewed_sources.negative_or_reverse_behavior: Released evidence before 1.118 remains immutable and out of the new budget; the evaluator never deletes, truncates, uploads, or rewrites evidence.

reviewed_sources.name: Generated workflow contract
reviewed_sources.notes: The workflow template records the new governance surfaces so initialized projects expose the same contract as the source repository.
reviewed_sources.lifecycle_reason: The workflow template is the declared entry contract consumed by project initialization.
reviewed_sources.expected_behavior: The generated workflow contract names the retention policy, checker, documentation, and shared library as required project surfaces.
reviewed_sources.negative_or_reverse_behavior: Template drift or omission must be detected by manifest validation and generated-project characterization tests.

reviewed_sources.name: Retention behavior characterization
reviewed_sources.processing_path_name: Positive, negative, historical, and generation-path verification
reviewed_sources.notes: Tests exercise accepted history, every governed rejection mode, read-only failure behavior, bounded Business Universe projection, and a real initialized project.
reviewed_sources.lifecycle_reason: The test suite is the audit surface that observes both intended behavior and fail-closed reverse paths.
reviewed_sources.expected_behavior: Valid forward-only retention state and generated-project state pass deterministic tests and strict checking.
reviewed_sources.negative_or_reverse_behavior: Multiple final runs, aggregate logs, duplicate raw evidence, exceeded budgets, and unbound runtime directories each fail with a specific finding while preserving inputs.
